question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's a complete `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm with all commonly required fields annotated:

```yaml
# -- Required top-level fields --
type: charm
name: my-k8s-app
summary: A one-line description (max 78 chars).
description: |
  A longer description used on Charmhub. Supports multiline YAML strings.

# Base OS the charm runs on. ubuntu@24.04 is standard.
base: ubuntu@24.04

# Target platforms/architectures
platforms:
  amd64:
    build-on: [amd64]
    build-for: [amd64]

# -- Kubernetes-specific: sidecar containers --
containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image      # must match a resource of type oci-image below
    mounts:
      - storage: data           # must match a storage entry below
        location: /var/lib/myapp

# OCI image resource (required for Kubernetes charms)
resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for my-app (e.g. hub.docker.com/_/my-app)

# -- Build configuration --
parts:
  my-charm:
    plugin: charm
    source: .
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

# -- Relations --
provides:
  http:
    interface: http
    optional: true

requires:
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true
  postgresql:
    interface: pgsql
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-peers
    optional: true

# -- User-facing config options --
config:
  options:
    log-level:
      default: info
      type: string
      description: Logging verbosity (debug, info, warning, error).
    port:
      default: 8080
      type: int
      description: Port the application listens on.

# -- Storage --
storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data directory.

# -- Actions --
actions:
  restart:
    description: Restart the application.

# -- Juju feature requirements --
assumes:
  - k8s-api           # required for Kubernetes charms
  - juju >= 3.3

# -- Charm libraries from Charmhub --
charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"

# -- Metadata --
title: My K8s App
links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-k8s-app
  source:
    - https://github.com/myorg/my-k8s-app-operator
  issues:
    - https://github.com/myorg/my-k8s-app-operator/issues
```

**Key Kubernetes-specific points:**

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `containers` | Defines sidecar containers. Each must reference an `oci-image` resource. |
| `resources` | Must have at least one `type: oci-image` entry per container. |
| `assumes: k8s-api` | Declares that the charm needs Kubernetes. Juju checks this before deployment. |
| `charm-user` | Optional. Accepts `root`, `sudoer`, or `non-root`. Currently only works on Kubernetes charms (requires Juju 3.6+). |
| `containers.*.mounts` | Links a storage entry to a path inside the container. |
| `storage` | For Kubernetes, storage entries used in container mounts should be `type: filesystem`. |

The only truly required fields are `type`, `name`, `summary`, `description`, `base`, `platforms`, `parts`, `containers`, and `resources` (with the OCI image). Everything else is optional but strongly recommended for a production charm.
